exercises.prompt: What is the imparfait used for?
exercises.explanation: Imparfait = how things were / used to be. A completed event uses the passé composé.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence describes a past HABIT (imparfait)?
exercises.explanation: « j'habitais » (imparfait) = used to live. 'j'ai déménagé' is a one-time event; 'je vais déménager' is the future.

exercises.prompt: Put être in the imparfait (when I WAS little).
exercises.template: Quand j'{{b1}} petit, j'avais un chien.
exercises.explanation: être in the imparfait: j'étais (irregular stem ét-).

exercises.prompt: Match each room/home word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order to say "My apartment has three rooms."
Listen, then: where did the speaker used to live?
exercises.transcript: Avant, j'habitais dans une petite maison à la campagne.

options.text: In a small house in the countryside

exercises.explanation: « j'habitais dans une petite maison à la campagne » = I used to live in a small house in the countryside.

exercises.prompt: Read and answer both questions. Notice the two past tenses.
exercises.passageFr: Quand j'étais enfant, j'habitais dans un grand appartement à Lyon. Il y avait trois chambres et un balcon. L'année dernière, j'ai déménagé à Paris.
exercises.passageEn: When I was a child, I lived in a big apartment in Lyon. There were three bedrooms and a balcony. Last year, I moved to Paris.

questions.prompt: As a child, where did the speaker live?

options.text: In a big apartment in Lyon
In a small house in Paris

exercises.prompt: Past habit → which tense? « When I was young, I lived in the countryside. »
exercises.template: Quand j'étais jeune, je {{b1}} à la campagne.
exercises.explanation: An ongoing past habit → imparfait (habitais). 'ai habité' would mark a finished, one-time stay.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetEn: Before, I used to live in Paris.
